items.title: Northern Ireland boss O'Neill signs four-year extension
items.summary: Northern Ireland manager Michael O'Neill has signed a four-year contract extension to keep him at the helm until 2032.

items.title: Spurs needed 'complete reset', says under fire CEO
items.summary: In a wide-ranging exclusive interview with BBC Sport, Tottenham chief executive Vinai Venkatesham talks about a number of issues the club needs to address.

items.title: 'Beginning of era' for young Barcelona team - Deco
items.summary: After back-to-back La Liga titles, sporting director Deco says a new era is only just beginning at La Liga champions Barcelona.

items.title: Celtic join chase for striker Rodriguez - gossip
items.summary: Celtic keen on La Liga striker as in-demand Kilmarnock midfielder opts to join English Championship newcomers...

items.title: 60 Palace 'troublemakers' ordered to leave Leipzig
items.summary: Police say 60 Crystal Palace fans classified as "known troublemakers" have been ordered to leave Leipzig while two arrests were made before the Europa Conference League final.

items.title: Which football club has had the fewest sponsor logos on their shirt? | The Knowledge
items.summary: Plus: biggest points deficit before a shock, worst-performing managers that helped win a title and keepers on corners Mail us with your all of your questions and answers “Which club has had the fewest sponsor logos adorn their shirts?” asks Bob Krag. “My guess is Arsenal, who have only had four (JVC

items.title: West End musical Wicked to open Women's T20 World Cup
items.summary: The 2026 Women's T20 World Cup is to get under way with a spectacular live performance from the cast of Wicked ahead of England's tournament opener against Sri Lanka on June 12.

items.title: Paul 'keen' for Ngannou fight or Fury rematch after return from injury
items.summary: Jake Paul would be "keen" for a fight with Francis Ngannou or a rematch with Tommy Fury should the boxer return successfully from a broken jaw, his business partner Nakisa Bidarian told Sky Sports.

items.title: The Sunderland footballer setting record straight after 46 years
items.summary: When Roly Gregoire quit football in 1980 after suffering a knee injury, he had faced so much racism that he could not bear to watch football for many years.

items.title: Rayo Vallecano take pride from the barrio into their fight for place in history
items.summary: Madrid’s third club thrive on underdog mentality and still play in the daring style of former coach Andoni Iraola “Rayo Vallecano is love, humility, toil,” says Óscar Trejo, the captain who handed in the armband in solidarity with workers at the club.
